summary: Drew DeVault shares his October 2020 progress update, covering work across several projects. He highlights major advances on SourceHut's GraphQL API 2.0, including database transaction support and a new async work processing library called dowork. He also details his deep dive into the Gemini protocol, having built a client, server, and HTTP-to-Gemini portal, plus a Gemini version of his blog. Additional updates include a Prometheus charting daemon and revival of the himitsu secret management project.
key_points: SourceHut's API 2.0 is nearing its first shippable state with writable GraphQL support, database transactions, and async webhook delivery
DeVault built four Gemini protocol projects in a single month — a client, line-mode browser, server, and HTTP-to-Gemini portal — demonstrating serious investment in the alternative protocol
He plans to post content exclusively on Gemini, signaling commitment to decentralized internet infrastructure beyond the web
New open-source tooling includes dowork (Go async processing) and chartsrv (Prometheus graphing daemon)
money_quotes: I've been getting more into Gemini this month, and have completed three (or four?) whole projects for it.
I'll be posting some content exclusively on Gemini (and I already have!), so get yourself a client if you want to tune in.
All of the main blockers for the first version of meta.sr.ht's writable GraphQL API are resolved, and after implementing a few more resolvers it should be in a shippable state.
I hope this new one lasts for another decade!
I also built a new general-purpose daemon for SourceHut called chartsrv, which can be used to generate graphs from Prometheus data.
key_insight: DeVault's prolific output across SourceHut infrastructure and Gemini protocol tooling reflects his commitment to building alternatives to corporate-controlled web platforms.
